title: pb-migrate project configuration
description: Schema for pb-migrate.json. The pb-migrate CLI reads this file to know which Pandorabots bots are managed by the project, where their AIML lives, and how to authenticate. String values support env-var substitution at load time using ${VAR} or ${VAR:-default} syntax; secrets should live in .env (gitignored), not in this file.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL. IDEs use this for autocomplete and validation.

host.description: Pandorabots API host. Defaults via env-var to https://api.pandorabots.com.

appId.description: Pandorabots application ID. Required. Typically supplied via ${PB_APP_ID} so the literal value lives in .env (gitignored), not this file.

userKey.description: Pandorabots user key. Required. Typically supplied via ${PB_USER_KEY}.

botKey.description: Optional bot key for atalk (anonymous talk). May be empty when atalk is not used.

bots.description: Map of bot name to per-bot configuration. The key is the bot name as it appears on Pandorabots; pb-migrate enforces that bot names are alphanumeric.

directory.description: Local directory containing the bot's AIML / set / map / substitution / pdefaults / properties files. Resolved relative to pb-migrate.json's location when relative.

files.description: Reserved for future glob-pattern filtering of files inside `directory`. Defaults to '*' (every file with a recognized extension).

propertiesUpload.description: How `.properties` files are uploaded. additive (default): PUT only — Pandorabots upserts and keys not in the local file are preserved on the server, allowing dashboard edits to coexist with pb-migrate. full: DELETE the remote properties file first, then PUT — local is authoritative and any keys not present locally are removed from the server.

alters.description: Persistent file-body overrides. Map of canonical file name (the name as it should appear on the bot) to a path of an override file. The override file's body is uploaded under the canonical name on every push, and its kind is inferred from the override path's extension. Designed for debug-session probes — temporary categories that dump predicates, simulate state, etc. — that should re-apply automatically while a session is active and be wiped with `alter:reset` when done. Manage with the alter:list / alter:set / alter:unset / alter:reset commands.

additionalProperties.description: Path to the override file. Relative paths are resolved against pb-migrate.json's directory.
